title: Stanza.
content:
If I walk in Autumn's even
    While the dead leaves pass,
    If I look on Spring's soft heaven, -
    Something is not there which was
    Winter's wondrous frost and snow,
    Summer's clouds, where are they now?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Percy Bysshe Shelley, titled "Stanza.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
